briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw changes in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — and proposed bylaw changes that would allow e-bike sharing and electric kick scooters on city streets and in parks. Council will also consider a range of other items affecting local businesses and housing.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, attend the April 14 meeting — Council will vote on the BIA renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby March 24, 2026 Meeting — Key Decisions on Housing, Zoning & Fees
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and property owners. These included rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, final adoption of updated parking meter and fees bylaws, a $4.2M water metering contract, and endorsement of a Poverty Reduction Strategy. Some items — including a modular housing pilot project — were sent back to staff for further review.
briefs.action_required: Check if any rezoning or bylaw change affects your property or business address, especially along Kingsway, Imperial Street, or Norland Avenue.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a special meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to the federal Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being shipped by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern stems from a nearby chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near Burnaby rail corridors, monitor council updates on chlorine transport safety decisions.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Day of Mourning Proclamation for Workers Killed or Injured on the Job
briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group is also asking Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14, 2026 meeting, encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th, and hear a 5-minute presentation on worker health and safety issues and WorkSafeBC (BC's workplace safety regulator) updates.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters affect your business, attend the April 14, 2026 Council meeting to hear the presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — proposed Burnaby proclamation date

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal, e-bike rules, and housing updates
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting. Key items include whether to proceed with renewing the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — plus proposed bylaw changes that would set rules for e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs on Burnaby streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, check the petition results — enough objections were received to potentially block the renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 election
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election, scheduled for October 17, 2026. The original officer appointed in September 2025 is on leave and is not expected to return in time, so a replacement is needed to keep election planning on track.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects who runs Burnaby's next local election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners opposed the renewal of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special tax zone where businesses collectively fund local improvements — to block it from moving forward. More than 50% of property owners by both parcel count (55%) and assessed value (52%) signed petitions against the renewal, meeting the legal threshold that prevents Council from adopting the renewal bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you are a North Road BIA member, contact the Association now — the BIA renewal has failed and funding stops April 1, 2026.
briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on annual housekeeping decisions for Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on routine annual corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — the City-owned company created to develop and manage affordable housing. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM) in favour of written resolutions, appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm December 12, 2026 as the official reference date, receive the BHA's 2025 financial statements, and formally appoint 10 directors to the BHA board for either two- or three-year terms.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this affects how Burnaby's affordable housing corporation is governed in 2026.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date — Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation 2026

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget of $1.96 million. Accepting these documents would unlock $2 million in City operating funding for the BHA, which is building 387 rental homes across two Burnaby sites.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — it could affect housing supply and rental availability in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: BHA 2026 operating funding release — council receipt meeting date
BHA $25 million GIC investment matures — financial planning milestone

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw changes
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to three Burnaby bylaws that would set the rules for a new E-Bike Share Pilot Program and bring the city in line with BC's Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program (which allows privately owned e-scooters on Burnaby streets). The proposed changes would define where e-bikes and e-scooters can ride and park, create designated parking zones for shared e-bikes, and add fines for riding these devices in areas not approved for their use.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's first housing target progress report to BC
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to send Burnaby's first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The province requires this report under the Housing Supply Act — a law that sets mandatory new-home building targets for cities. Burnaby must build 10,240 net new homes by August 2030, and this report covers the first six months of progress (Sept 2025–Feb 2026), during which about 910 new units were completed.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — housing targets may affect development timelines and rental supply in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Year 1b Housing Target Progress Report submission deadline — BC Housing Supply Act

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby continuing Building Benchmark BC energy tracking program
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing Burnaby's continued participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 in 2026. This voluntary program helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions — at no extra cost to the City, as the roughly $16,500 contribution fits within the existing Planning and Development budget.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, watch for the City's BBBC recruitment outreach — joining is free and gives you energy data.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas works at Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on giving FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way — a legal permission to access and use a strip of city-owned land — over parts of two properties near Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other park facilities in the area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain or relies on FortisBC gas service there.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit residential building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey building would be city-owned and rented out, with the goal of testing whether this style of construction is a cost-effective way to add housing in lower-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting — this decision could shape future modular housing contracts in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award public meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract to replace Jerry Rogers Creek culvert in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert (a pipe or tunnel that carries water under a road or structure) at Jerry Rogers Creek with a larger box culvert built to handle major flood flows. The project also includes replacing nearby water and sewer lines.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting if your business is near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive — construction could affect access.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) and complete final paving along the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The project is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway contract award public meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension with Rogers for city cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $450,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. to keep the city's cellular voice and data services running while it switches to a new provider. The city ran a public bidding process for a new mobile services contract and is still finalizing that deal, so this extension would bridge the gap and prevent any disruption to city operations.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is an internal city contract with no impact on private businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract extension for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its existing contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. to continue heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance and repair at city-owned buildings. The proposed contract change is worth $579,600 including taxes and is meant as a short-term bridge while the city runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term service provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects HVAC service contracts with the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — HVAC contract change approval meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software license contract with Long View Systems Corporation for approximately $394,942 (taxes included). The license keeps the City's phone-based customer service centre running and supports IT staff — it is not a new contract with a small business, but owners who rely on City services should know this renewal runs from May 2026 to May 2029.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business depends on Burnaby's phone-based City services.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Cisco WebEx Contact Center contract renewal — council vote
Burnaby WebEx Contact Center renewed contract period takes effect

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Renewing fire protection services contract for $1.67M
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, repair, and maintain fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes — with the vendor holding its existing pricing.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business supplies or competes in fire protection services.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal — proposed start date
Burnaby Council meeting — fire protection contract renewal vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on external financial review and North Road BIA renewal outcome
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including hiring an outside reviewer to check the City's financial practices and a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. Council will also consider the results of a petition process that could block the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements.
briefs.action_required: If you operate in the North Road BIA, attend the April 14 Council meeting to follow the BIA renewal vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal vote and financial review decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on bringing in an outside expert to review how the City manages its finances, plans spending, and sets priorities for major projects. The goal is to protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of significant tax hikes or higher service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — it could affect future tax and service cost levels.

key_dates.label: Burnaby external financial review — Council vote takes place

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street Up for Council Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street in the Royal Oak area. The current site holds a printing shop and is zoned for industrial use — the proposed rezoning would change that to allow mid-rise residential development.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council meeting notices for the First and Second Reading date to track this rezoning's progress.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to Consider North Road BIA Renewal and Economic Growth Task Force Update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including a proposed renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses pool funds for area improvements — and an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Council will also consider a report that a developer has chosen to forfeit (give up) security money rather than build a public amenity pier at a Burnaby riverfront site.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, watch the April 14 Council vote — the BIA renewal outcome directly affects your area levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby City Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal vote and consent agenda

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to identify ways to strengthen Burnaby's local economy. The Task Force is still mid-process — it has held five workshops so far and is set to deliver final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council
Mayor's Task Force reconvenes — Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Public Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive information about a developer's decision to pay $1,363,500 to the City of Burnaby rather than build a public pier on the Fraser River. Oxford Properties could not get the required provincial land approval to build the pier at Riverbend Business Park, so under a legal agreement (called a Section 219 Covenant — a registered promise tied to the property title), they are allowed to hand over the security deposit instead. Council will consider how to use those funds for a future public amenity.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's future decision on how the $1.36M will be spent — it may fund nearby public amenities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier Covenant report received for information

briefs.title: Council to Consider Information Reports — Burnaby Meeting April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review information reports at its April 14, 2026 meeting. No body text or details were provided for this agenda item, so the specific topics under consideration are not yet known.

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal — but sufficient objections received
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to consider final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pay a levy to fund local improvements — for a 5-year period. However, the agenda notes that enough property owners have submitted formal petitions against the renewal to potentially block it, so the outcome is uncertain.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, attend the April 14 Council meeting to follow the vote on the renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal Bylaw #14797 — Council final adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on dissolving Burnaby North Road BIA renewal after sufficient petitions received
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses collectively fund local improvements — for another 5 years. However, the City has received enough signed petitions against the renewal to potentially block it, and Council will consider this at the April 14, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, attend the April 14 Council meeting to follow the vote outcome.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — Council final adoption vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area Bylaw — Council to vote on final adoption
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would officially create the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool funds for local improvements like street beautification, graffiti removal, and business promotion. If approved, property owners in the zone with light industrial or commercial properties would pay a special local tax to fund the BIA, totalling up to $1.75 million over five years (April 2026–March 2031). Important note: enough property owners have formally objected to trigger a reconsideration, which means the bylaw's passage is not guaranteed.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property on Burnaby North Road, contact City Hall now — enough objections were filed to force a reconsideration vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — Council final adoption vote
Burnaby North Road BIA — proposed start date if bylaw is adopted
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline to City
Burnaby North Road BIA — proposed five-year term end date

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: No Action Items: Burnaby Council Correspondence Package — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: The April 14, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting included a correspondence section, but no items of correspondence were submitted for Council to decide on. All other correspondence received has been published in a separate public package dated the same day.

briefs.title: Council to Consider North Road BIA Renewal and Multiple City Contracts at April 14 Meeting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pay a levy to fund local improvements — for another five years. Council will also consider contract awards, bylaw changes for e-bikes and electric kick scooters, and an external review of the City's financial practices.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, check the petition results — enough objections were received to affect the renewal vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — Council vote date

briefs.title: Council to Announce 2026 Bursary Awards for Eight Burnaby Secondary School Students
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on granting bursary awards (one-time financial grants) to eight secondary school students across Burnaby schools. If approved, staff would notify the recipients and their school principals, with the awards announced publicly at the April 14, 2026 Open Council meeting.
